fix(monaco): preserve Markdown font styles (bold, italic, strikethrou…
prs.body:
…gh) (#1022)

<!-- DO NOT IGNORE THE TEMPLATE!

Thank you for contributing!

Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following:

- Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- Check that there isn't already a PR that solves the problem the same way to avoid creating a duplicate.
- Provide a description in this PR that addresses **what** the PR is solving, or reference the issue that it solves (e.g. `fixes #123`).
- Ideally, include relevant tests that fail without this PR but pass with it.

-->

### Description
This PR fixes an issue where shikiToMonaco failed to preserve Markdown text styles (bold, italic, strikethrough) when integrating Shiki themes into the Monaco Editor.
Previously, Monaco tokens were mapped only by color, which caused loss of font-style metadata.
Now, styles like bold, italic, and strikethrough are correctly rendered across all supported themes.

### Summary of Changes

* Added `normalizeFontStyleString()` and `normalizeFontStyleBits()` to handle Shiki → Monaco font style mapping.
* Implemented dual mapping for:

  * `color → scope` (backward compatibility)
  * `color + style → scope` (for accurate style preservation)
* Updated `textmateThemeToMonacoTheme()` to forward `fontStyle` correctly.
* Simplified theme registration logic and removed redundant mappings.
* Verified behavior for Markdown tokens (`markup.bold`, `markup.italic`, `markup.strikethrough`).

---

### Linked Issue

Fixes #1022

---
<!-- Please insert your description here and provide especially info about the "what" this PR is solving -->



### Testing & Verification

* **Env:** `monaco-editor@0.54.0`, Shiki themes `github-dark` / `github-light`.
* **API Check:**

  ```js
  editor._themeService._knownThemes.get('github-dark').tokenTheme._match('markup.bold')
  // → _fontStyle: 2 (bold)
  ```
* **Result:**
  **Bold**, *italic*, and ~~strikethrough~~ now render correctly; no regressions found.
* **Console:**

  ```
  Style applied: markup.bold bold
  Style applied: markup.italic italic
  Style applied: markup.strikethrough strikethrough
  ```


### Additional context
Verified theme switching (github-dark ↔ github-light) works correctly.

No regressions observed in syntax highlighting for other languages.

The change aligns Monaco’s highlighting fidelity with Shiki’s output.
<!-- e.g. is there anything you'd like reviewers to focus on? -->

prs.title: fix(monaco): preserve bold, italic, and strikethrough token styles in…
prs.body:
### Description

This PR fixes an issue where `shikiToMonaco` didn’t preserve Markdown text styles (**bold**, *italic*, ~~strikethrough~~) when using Shiki themes in the Monaco Editor.
Previously, Monaco only mapped tokens by color, losing style metadata.
Now, bold, italic, underline, and strikethrough styles are correctly applied across all supported themes.

### Summary of Changes

* Added `parseFontStyleToMask()` to translate Shiki `fontStyle` strings into Monaco bitmasks.
* Introduced dual mapping:
  `color → scope` (backward compatibility) and `color + style → scope` (accurate style mapping).
* Updated theme conversion to propagate `fontStyle`.
* Removed unused helpers and ensured ESLint compliance.

### Linked Issue

Fixes #1022

### Testing & Verification

Tested locally with `monaco-editor@0.54.0` and Shiki themes `github-dark` / `github-light`.
Confirmed via Monaco’s API that Markdown tokens (`markup.bold`, `markup.italic`, `markup.strikethrough`) now return correct `fontStyle` values.
Visually verified that bold, italic, and strikethrough render correctly, with no regressions in other languages.

Console logs confirmed:

```
Style applied: markup.bold bold
Style applied: markup.italic italic
Style applied: markup.strikethrough strikethrough
```

### Additional Context

Verified theme switching works correctly.
All lint, type, and build checks pass.

prs.title: Feat/monaco preserve font styles
prs.body:
<!-- DO NOT IGNORE THE TEMPLATE!

Thank you for contributing!

Before submitting the PR, please make sure you do the following:

- Read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/shikijs/shiki/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- Check that there isn't already a PR that solves the problem the same way to avoid creating a duplicate.
- Provide a description in this PR that addresses **what** the PR is solving, or reference the issue that it solves (e.g. `fixes #123`).
- Ideally, include relevant tests that fail without this PR but pass with it.

-->

### Description
Preserve font styles (bold/italic/underline) in Monaco when using Shiki themes.

- Update shikiToMonaco to prefer styled scopes when a token’s metadata indicates a font style.
- This ensures Markdown emphasis and similar styled tokens render correctly in Monaco.

Fixes #1022
What’s changed

- packages/monaco/src/index.ts
- Track two mappings per color: general scope and “styled” scope (has fontStyle).
- For tokens with non-zero fontStyle, prefer the styled-scope; otherwise fall back to general scope.
- packages/monaco/test/preserve-font-style.test.ts
- Ensures textmateThemeToMonacoTheme(vitesse-light) includes styled rules for markdown-like (markup) scopes.

Rationale

- Monaco supports only one scope per token. Previously, we mapped color → first scope, losing style info. By preferring a scope that declares fontStyle for styled tokens, Monaco can render emphasis per theme.

Verification

- Automated:
- New test checks that Monaco theme derived from Vitesse contains bold/italic rules for markup scopes.
- Manual (recommended):
- Use a theme with styled rules (e.g., vitesse-light/vitesse-dark) and markdown.
- Run the Monaco playground, confirm bold/italic/underline render as expected.


<!-- Please insert your description here and provide especially info about the "what" this PR is solving -->

### Linked Issues
fixes #1022

### Additional context
No runtime API changes.
Behavior is additive and backwards compatible.
Applies beyond Markdown wherever font-styled TextMate rules exist.

<!-- e.g. is there anything you'd like reviewers to focus on? -->

prs.title: Feature/element guessing game v2
prs.body: Build an interactive Periodic Table guessing game using React.js (or Vanilla JS) where players identify chemical elements based on given clues like group, period, category, or atomic number. The game can either fetch real-time element data from a free Periodic Table API or use a local JSON dataset. - Players receive immediate feedback (correct/incorrect) and earn points for each correct answer. Add features like: - Hints for tough elements (e.g., reveal symbol or category). - Timer-based rounds for added challenge. - Animations for smooth transitions and engaging visuals. A leaderboard to track high scores locally.

prs.title: Add comprehensive documentation and single command startup script
prs.body:
Closes #1

This PR addresses the need for better documentation and a single command to locally test the entire project:

## Changes Made:
- Created comprehensive README.md with detailed setup and usage instructions
- Added a single command script (start-youedit.sh) that:
  - Starts all backend services (app.js, serve.js, tus.js, youtube.js)
  - Launches the frontend development server
  - Creates log files for each service
  - Includes a companion stop-youedit.sh script for easy shutdown

## How to Test:
1. Clone the repository
2. Run `chmod +x start-youedit.sh` to make the script executable
3. Run `./start-youedit.sh` to start all services
4. Verify that all services start correctly
5. Use `./stop-youedit.sh` to stop all services

These improvements make the repository more user-friendly and easier to set up for local testing.

prs.title: fix: show PowerShell PATH instructions for Windows users
prs.body:
##  Problem

Goose CLI install script shows **Unix-style PATH instructions** on Windows (e.g., `.bashrc`, `.zshrc`).
This confuses Windows users, who primarily use **PowerShell**.
##  Before

```text
Warning: goose installed, but /home/rob-admin/.local/bin is not in your PATH.
Add it to your PATH by editing ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, or similar...
```
##  After

```text
Warning: goose installed, but C:\Users\<user>\.local\bin is not in your PATH.
To add goose to your PATH in PowerShell:

$profilePath = $PROFILE
if (!(Test-Path $profilePath)) { New-Item -Path $profilePath -ItemType File -Force }
Add-Content -Path $profilePath -Value '$env:PATH = "$env:USERPROFILE\.local\bin;$env:PATH"'
. $PROFILE

Alternatively: goose configure
```
##  Fix

* Detect Windows and show **PowerShell instructions**.
* Keep Bash/Zsh for Unix systems.
* Added alternatives: `goose configure` or rerun script.
##  Benefit

Clearer setup for Windows users → **less confusion, smoother onboarding**.

prs.title: Fix: Properly reset used_riddles in assign_riddle function
prs.body:
- The assign_riddle() function did not properly reset used_riddles when all riddles were exhausted. This caused an IndexError due to an empty list in random.choice(), leading to potential infinite loops or unexpected behavior.
- 
- **Changes Made:**
- Fixed riddle exhaustion issue by ensuring used_riddles resets when all riddles have been attempted.
-  Improved logic for retrieving available riddles without errors.
-  Handled edge cases, ensuring no crashes when assigning riddles.

** Testing & Edge Cases Covered:**
-  Tested via test_riddles.py, which includes:
- Riddle Assignment: Assigns riddles correctly.
- Riddle Exhaustion: Resets used_riddles when all riddles are used.
- Empty Riddle List: Handles cases where no riddles are available.

issues.title: Register / Login Endpoints
issues.body:
**Description:**  
Implement secure authentication endpoints for Smart Course Registration System.

**Endpoints:**
- POST /api/auth/register — Register a new user (Student, Faculty, or Admin)
- POST /api/auth/login — Authenticate and return JWT token

**Requirements:**
- Validate input fields (email, password length ≥ 6)
- Ensure email uniqueness
- Hash passwords using bcrypt before saving
- Return JWT token on successful login
- Use environment variables for secret keys

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] User can register with valid data
- [ ] User can log in successfully
- [ ] Passwords are hashed in the database
- [ ] Invalid credentials handled gracefully
- [ ] JWT returned upon successful login
- [ ] All endpoints tested via Postman

issues.title: Editable Projects + Delete Option + Team Size Auto-Archive Logic
issues.body:
### 1.  Editable Project Post 

**Allow editing project details after creation**, just like how profile editing works.

 Add a **" Edit Project"** button beside "Invite" in the **My Projects** list (or project card).

#### Editable Fields:
- Project title
- Description
- Stack / tech used
- Roles or required skills
- Team size
- Timeline etc


### 2.  Delete Project Option 

** Enable project owners to delete their own project**.

 Add a **" Delete"** option with confirmation prompt:

- **Soft Delete (Recommended):** Add `isDeleted: true`
  - Keeps data for potential audit
  - Prevents broken invites/collaborations

### 3.  Team Size Tracking 

After someone accepts an invite and becomes a collaborator, track team progress.

#### Dynamic Display
- Example:  
  `"Team size: 2 / 5 members joined"`

####  Manual "Archive Project"
- Add toggle: **"Mark as Full"** or **"Archive Project"**
- Updates status: `isOpen: false`
- Project stops appearing in Explore

issues.title: Explore Projects Page (Public View)
issues.body:
Build a public-facing page to display all project ideas that are marked as Open for Collaboration(include this option in post a project form as it is not included yet). This enables users to browse available projects and express interest in joining.

Since backend integration is not completed yet, we'll simulate the behavior using dummy data and console logs.
Create a new `/explore-projects` route with a page that displays project cards using local dummy data. This will allow users to browse open projects and show interest (console log for now).

## Requirements
- [ ] Create new route `/explore-projects` (page: `ExploreProjects.jsx`)
- [ ] Use local dummy data to simulate project cards
- [ ] Each project card should display:
  - Project Title
  - Short Description
  - Tech Stack
  - Status (e.g., "Open for Collaboration")
  - Roles needed (tags or simple text)
  - "Show Interest" button (console.log click)
- [ ] Design clean responsive layout
- [ ] Add loading state view
- [ ] Add empty state view
- [ ] Include one hard-coded demo project as example
- [ ] Reuse existing styling where possible
- [ ] Add dummy checkbox in Post a Project form for "Open for Collaboration"

links like github

issues.title: Create Navbar Component and Integrate with Global Layout
issues.body:
Build a reusable and responsive **Navbar** component that will appear on **every page** of the application. The Navbar should be integrated into the existing `Layout.jsx` wrapper located in `src/layout/`.

###  Acceptance Criteria:

- [ ] Include links to:
  - `Landing` 
  - `Explore` 
  -  my projects 
  - `Post Project` 
  - `Messages`
  - `Profile`
- [ ] Add a placeholder **logo** 
- [ ] Add **profile icon** 

 follow our refrence template

- Use **Tailwind CSS**
- Follow the **dark modern UI** reference
- Keep it clean and minimal for now; enhancements can come later

issues.title: Create Post a Project Page
issues.body:
Build the **Post a Project** screen for users to submit their project ideas and start forming teams. This form is central to H@ckollab's core functionality and should be user-friendly, responsive, and clearly structured.

This form allows users to:
- Share their project idea
- Specify tech & team requirements
- Invite collaborators later

For now, it can be built using **dummy data / local state** without backend API integration.
###  Acceptance Criteria:

####  Basic Information Section:
- [ ] **Project Title** (Text input)
- [ ] **Description** 

####  Collaboration Details:
- [ ] **Project Status** 
- [ ] **Collaboration Type** 

####  Technical Requirements:
- [ ] **Tech Stack Needed**
  - Allow custom tech input

####  Difficulty Level:
- [ ] **Select difficulty** 

####  Team Requirements:
- [ ] **Roles Open** (Multi-select tags)
  - Allow custom role input
- [ ] **Maximum Team Size** 

#### Additional Details:
- [ ] **Deadline **
- [ ] **Project Tags** (Multi-tag input:)


####  Submission Button:
- [ ] `Post Project` button
  - Show success  (no actual backend yet)

issues.title: Create Explore Page UI
issues.body:
Build the UI for the Explore page to show available developers. Use dummy UserCard components for now.
usercard should have developer name , description , skills , invite button , hiss github link . create upto 5-6 user cards using dummy data .

Use placeholder buttons (onClick={() => {}})

No routing or backend needed.

issues.title: Project Model & CRUD APIs

milestone.title: Projects & Invites
milestone.description: Users can create projects and invite teammates

issues.body:
Design the Prisma model for Project and build backend CRUD APIs for posting and exploring projects.
**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Define Project model in schema.prisma
- [ ] Fields like: id, title, description, tags, status, techStack, maxTeamSize, createdBy (User)
- [ ] Migrate with npx prisma migrate dev
- [ ] API Endpoints:
- [ ] POST /api/projects: create a new project
- [ ] GET /api/projects: fetch all projects
- [ ] GET /api/projects/:id: fetch single project by ID
- [ ] Include creator ID relation (createdBy) via Clerk or hardcoded (for now)
- [ ] Add 2–3 seed projects (optional)

issues.title: Implement Skill-Based Filtering for /api/users
issues.body:
Enhance the /api/users route to support filtering by tech stack (skills).
Example query: /api/users?stack=react,node
**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Match users having at least one of those skills
- [ ] Return filtered list of users
- [ ] (Optional) Add basic pagination support like ?page=1&limit=10

issues.title: Clerk Authentication Integration

milestone.title: core setup and auth
milestone.description: Set up the frontend/backend architecture and authentication

issues.body:
Set up Clerk for user login/signup via email + OAuth (Google, GitHub). Protect routes using useUser() hook.
### Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Form includes: name, bio, GitHub, portfolio, skills (tags), availability toggle
- [ ] Includes basic validation (required fields)
- [ ] Submits data to `/api/users`
- [ ] Shows success and error feedback
- [ ] Responsive layout on mobile and desktop
